lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Hotel Check-in
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marlene Landucci | Aula 11 -- Hotel Check-in

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to the Grand Plaza Hotel. Thank you for choosing to stay with us tonight. The reception desk is open twenty-four hours a day, so you can call us at any time. Breakfast is served in the lobby restaurant from seven to ten in the morning. The elevator is on your right, next to the front desk. Your room is on the third floor, and it has a beautiful view of the city. The Wi-Fi password is on your key card. We hope you enjoy your stay with us. If you need anything, please come to the front desk. Have a wonderful evening.

Hi, this is a message for Marlene. This is the front desk calling. I am happy to tell you that your room is ready now. You have a double room on the third floor, with a beautiful view of the park. Your key card is waiting for you here at reception. Breakfast is in the lobby tomorrow morning. Please remember to sign the form when you arrive. We are very happy to have you with us. Thank you, and see you soon.

extra_audio.text: First, you arrive at the hotel and walk into the lobby. Then you go to the front desk and say you have a reservation. After that, the receptionist asks for your name and your passport. Next, you sign the form and take your key card. Finally, you take the elevator to your floor and find your room.
